questions.q: The Revolt of 1857 began with the mutiny of sepoys in which town on 10 May 1857?
questions.explanation: The uprising broke out at Meerut on 10 May 1857.

questions.q: Against whose rule was the Revolt of 1857 primarily directed?
questions.explanation: The revolt was directed against the rule of the British East India Company.

questions.q: By which year was the Revolt of 1857 largely suppressed?
questions.explanation: The uprising was largely crushed by 1858.

questions.q: The Revolt of 1857 is often described by Indian nationalists by which name?
questions.explanation: It is often called the First War of Independence in Indian nationalist historiography.

questions.q: An immediate cause of the revolt was the introduction of which firearm's controversial cartridges?
questions.explanation: The greased cartridges of the new Enfield rifle were a major immediate trigger of the revolt.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor was proclaimed the symbolic leader of the 1857 rebels at Delhi?
questions.explanation: The rebels rallied around Bahadur Shah Zafar, the last Mughal emperor, in Delhi.

questions.q: What major political change followed the suppression of the 1857 revolt?
options: The founding of the Indian National Congress
The end of Company rule and transfer of power to the British Crown
questions.explanation: The revolt led to the end of East India Company rule and the transfer of power to the British Crown.

questions.q: The 1857 revolt was concentrated mainly in which regions of India?
questions.explanation: The uprising spread chiefly across northern and central India.

questions.q: Which queen became a celebrated leader of the resistance during the 1857 revolt?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai, the Queen of Jhansi, was a leading figure of the 1857 resistance.

questions.q: The participants in the 1857 mutiny were primarily soldiers known by what name?
questions.explanation: The Indian soldiers of the Company army who rebelled were called sepoys.

questions.q: Which British annexation policy, which absorbed states lacking a natural male heir, was a key cause of the 1857 revolt?
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie's Doctrine of Lapse, used to annex states without a direct male heir, was a major grievance.

questions.q: The annexation of which kingdom in 1856 deeply angered its population and contributed to the revolt?
questions.explanation: The annexation of Awadh (Oudh) in 1856 on grounds of misgovernment caused widespread resentment.

questions.q: Who led the rebellion at Kanpur during the Revolt of 1857?
questions.explanation: Nana Saheb led the uprising at Kanpur, supported by associates such as Tatya Tope.

questions.q: Who was the Governor-General of India when the 1857 revolt broke out?
questions.explanation: Lord Canning was Governor-General during the revolt and oversaw the transition to Crown rule.

questions.q: Which leader of the revolt in Bihar, an aged zamindar, is celebrated for his resistance at Jagdishpur?
questions.explanation: Veer Kunwar Singh of Jagdishpur in Bihar was a prominent leader of the revolt in his region.

questions.q: In Awadh, who organised resistance at Lucknow on behalf of her young son during the revolt?
questions.explanation: Begum Hazrat Mahal led the rebel cause at Lucknow on behalf of her son Birjis Qadr.

questions.q: Which 1858 legislation formally transferred the government of India from the Company to the Crown?
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act 1858 ended Company rule and placed India under the British Crown.

questions.q: Which city saw a prolonged and famous siege of the British Residency during the 1857 revolt?
questions.explanation: The Siege of the Lucknow Residency was one of the most prolonged engagements of the revolt.

questions.q: After the revolt, in 1858 Queen Victoria issued a Proclamation that promised what to the people of India?
questions.explanation: The 1858 Proclamation promised non-interference in religion and equal protection under the law, among other assurances.

questions.q: Which experienced rebel general commanded the sepoy forces in Delhi during the revolt?
questions.explanation: Bakht Khan emerged as a leading military commander of the rebel forces in Delhi.

questions.q: What happened to the British administrative structure when the Crown took over, replacing the Company's Board?
options: The post of Viceroy was abolished
A Secretary of State for India was created
questions.explanation: The 1858 Act created the office of Secretary of State for India and converted the Governor-General into the Viceroy.

questions.q: Tatya Tope, a key military leader of the revolt, was a close associate of which Kanpur leader?
questions.explanation: Tatya Tope was a close associate and commander under Nana Saheb.

questions.q: The Revolt of 1857 is sometimes said to have a symbolic earlier spark at Barrackpore on 29 March 1857. What event was that?
options: Mangal Pandey's attack on his officers
questions.explanation: Mangal Pandey's revolt at Barrackpore on 29 March 1857 preceded and foreshadowed the wider uprising.

questions.q: Who was the rebel religious leader, known as the 'Maulvi of Faizabad', prominent in the revolt in Awadh?
questions.explanation: Maulvi Ahmadullah Shah of Faizabad was a notable rebel leader in the Awadh region.

questions.q: Which British general led the decisive assault that recaptured Delhi from the rebels in September 1857?
options: Operations associated with John Nicholson and Archdale Wilson
questions.explanation: The recapture of Delhi was associated with John Nicholson (who was killed in the assault) and the force under Archdale Wilson.

questions.q: Which British general led the campaign in central India that defeated the rebels at Jhansi and Gwalior in 1858?
questions.explanation: Sir Hugh Rose commanded the Central India Field Force that captured Jhansi and later Gwalior in 1858.

questions.q: V. D. Savarkar's influential 1909 book that interpreted the revolt as a national war carried which title?
questions.explanation: Savarkar's 'The Indian War of Independence, 1857' framed the revolt as India's first national war of independence.

questions.q: Following the revolt, the British announced a policy regarding the princely states that did what?
options: Abandoned the Doctrine of Lapse and recognised adoption
questions.explanation: The Crown abandoned the Doctrine of Lapse and recognised the rulers' right of adoption, seeking the loyalty of the princes.

questions.q: After the revolt, how did the British change the composition of the Indian army to guard against future mutiny?
options: They abolished the Indian army entirely
They raised the proportion of European to Indian soldiers and recruited along 'martial race' lines
questions.explanation: The army was reorganised with a higher ratio of British troops and recruitment increasingly favoured groups deemed 'martial races'.

questions.q: Which last Mughal emperor was tried by the British after the revolt and exiled, ending the dynasty?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah Zafar was tried, exiled to Rangoon, and the Mughal dynasty came to an end.
